title: hibiscus clypeatus subsp. membranaceus
intro: Hibiscus clypeatus subsp. membranaceus, also known as the Yellow Hibiscus or Tropical Hibiscus, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive, almost translucent, membranous calyx. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Hibiscus clypeatus subsp. membranaceus include lowering blood pressure, reducing cholesterol levels, and aiding in the treatment of various digestive issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
